---
title: "Question 1 - Latin Square Design ANOVA (Wheat varieties)"
author: "Upayan Mazumder"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
alpha <- 0.05
```

## Question

Analyze and interpret the output data for wheat varieties A, B, C and D under a Latin Square Design.

```{r data}
ls_df <- data.frame(
  Row = factor(rep(c("R1", "R2", "R3", "R4"), each = 4)),
  Column = factor(rep(c("C1", "C2", "C3", "C4"), times = 4)),
  Variety = factor(c(
    "C", "B", "A", "D",
    "A", "D", "C", "B",
    "B", "A", "D", "C",
    "D", "C", "B", "A"
  )),
  Yield = c(
    25, 23, 20, 20,
    19, 19, 21, 18,
    19, 14, 17, 20,
    17, 20, 21, 15
  )
)

ls_df
aggregate(Yield ~ Variety, data = ls_df, FUN = mean)
```

## ANOVA for Latin Square Design

```{r anova}
fit <- aov(Yield ~ Row + Column + Variety, data = ls_df)
anova_table <- summary(fit)
anova_table
```

```{r decision}
p_variety <- anova_table[[1]]["Variety", "Pr(>F)"]

if (p_variety < alpha) {
  cat("Decision: Reject H0 at 5% significance level.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: Wheat variety has a significant effect on yield.")
} else {
  cat("Decision: Fail to reject H0 at 5% significance level.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: No significant difference among variety means is detected.")
}
```
